severity_description.Early: Small olive-green spots on leaves. Limited spread.
severity_description.Moderate: Multiple dark scab lesions. Some leaf distortion.
severity_description.Advanced: Heavy scabbing, leaf drop, fruit affected.

treatment: Apply fungicide containing captan or myclobutanil
Remove and destroy infected leaves immediately
Improve air circulation by pruning
Avoid overhead irrigation
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Apply preventive fungicide sprays in spring before symptoms appear

severity_description.Moderate: Expanding brown lesions with purple border.

treatment: Remove infected branches and mummified fruit
Apply copper-based fungicide
Prune dead wood during dry weather
Disinfect pruning tools between cuts
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Maintain tree vigor through proper fertilization and watering

severity_description.Early: Yellow-orange spots on upper leaf surface.
severity_description.Moderate: Spots enlarge with orange spore tubes below.

treatment: Apply fungicide with myclobutanil or propiconazole
Remove nearby juniper or cedar trees if possible
Begin sprays at pink bud stage
No treatment needed — plant appears healthy
Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring and preventive care

severity_description.Early: Small tan rectangular lesions on lower leaves.
severity_description.Moderate: Lesions expand, merge on multiple leaves.

treatment: Apply fungicide with strobilurin or triazole
Increase plant spacing for better airflow
Rotate crops — avoid continuous maize planting
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Use resistant hybrids and practice crop rotation

severity_description.Early: Small pustules on both leaf surfaces.

treatment: Apply fungicide with propiconazole
Scout fields regularly during growing season
Apply at first sign of disease

severity_description.Early: Small water-soaked lesions on lower leaves.
severity_description.Advanced: Lesions cover most leaf area, severe blighting.

treatment: Apply foliar fungicide at early disease onset
Use strobilurin-based fungicides for best control
Remove heavily infected plant debris
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Choose resistant hybrids and rotate crops

treatment: No treatment needed — plant appears healthy
Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Continue regular scouting and crop management

severity_description.Early: Small dark spots with concentric rings on older leaves.

treatment: Apply fungicide with chlorothalonil or mancozeb
Remove infected lower leaves immediately
Avoid wetting foliage when irrigating
Apply mulch to prevent soil splash
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Use disease-free seeds and practice crop rotation

severity_description.Early: Water-soaked pale green spots on leaves.
severity_description.Moderate: Brown lesions with white mold on undersides.
severity_description.Advanced: Rapid collapse of foliage and fruit rot.

treatment: Apply copper-based or mancozeb fungicide immediately
Remove and destroy all infected plant material
Do not compost infected material
Improve field drainage
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties and avoid overhead irrigation

treatment: No treatment needed — plant appears healthy
